name: electroplankton-decomp
title: Chaos Viewer
tagline: matching C from the chaos — Electroplankton
compiler: mwccarm 1.2/sp2p3. Flags (C): -O4,p -enum int -lang c99 -char signed -interworking -proc arm946e -gccext,on -msgstyle gcc
cppNote: If a name starts with _Z (C++ mangled), write C++ with the FIRST LINE exactly //cpp
setup: clone {github} and follow CONTRIBUTING.md (python deps + mwccarm under tools/mwccarm/ gitignored + unpack YOUR OWN cartridge dump with dsd / tools/unpack.py). Optional: ./tools/setup_vendor.sh for decomp-permuter + m2c. Never commit the ROM or extracts.
readFirst: notes/matching-style.md (C not asm; matching loop). notes/mwccarm-codegen.md (esp. sec 6e-6g levers: u64-mask laundering for materialized bases, declaration/statement order for register coloring, //cpp dummy-vtable dispatch, struct-copy interleave) and notes/pret-idioms.md — same spine as sm64ds-decomp. notes/compiler.md (1.2/sp2p3 pin + flags). notes/nearmiss.md (best tip C in nearmiss/db.jsonl). notes/match-attempts.md + notes/match-provenance.md (experimental attempt tree + how-record). notes/link-verification.md (linkcheck / wrong-callee trap). notes/ghidra-scaffolds.md (local decompiler dumps). tools/permuter/README.md when near-miss is coloring/ordering-blocked. CONTRIBUTING.md, notes/legal.md. Coordinate via CLAIMS.md. Iteration oracles: tools/fdiff.py (per-insn), tools/disasm.py / tools/abrow.py (full annotated listing when prompt truncates).
rules: only your own legally dumped ROM; never commit the ROM, its assets, or extracted binaries — the one deliberate exception is the annotated disassembly TEXT of still-unmatched functions on the chaos-data branch (text, not bytes/assets), same practice as decomp repos committing .s files; import symbol/struct knowledge (see CREDITS.md) but write all C from scratch; deliverable is C/C++ that goes through mwccarm then tools/match.py — never submit straight assembly as a match; target disassembly is the oracle to match, not source to paste. After MATCH, stamp provenance with tools/bank.py (EP experimental); near-miss tips go to nearmiss/db.jsonl via tools/log_attempt.py --src (or nearmiss_db), not into src/ as fake matches. For call-heavy functions prefer tools/linkcheck.py / match --strict-relocs before PR (byte match wildcards reloc slots).
nearMissNote: If a function will not fully match after real effort, do not discard your best attempt — it is still valuable. Keep compiling C and log with tools/log_attempt.py --status near_miss --divergences N --src yourfile.c so the tip is upserted into nearmiss/db.jsonl (full C of the closest draft, sm64ds-shaped). Also record every try (including no_progress) in config/match_attempts.jsonl with functionId + unique attemptId + parentAttemptId. Iterate the tip with tools/fdiff.py; if the gap is regalloc/ordering only, tools/permuter/crunch.py or import_func + vendor/decomp-permuter can finish for free. Prefer PR'ing near-miss C over abandoning to asm. // NONMATCHING: <what differs> (div=N) still marks parked src drafts.
